title: Brucella species detection from blood and milk samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4a0b5","text":"Brucellosis is a widespread zoonotic disease which is characterized by ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ioap","text":"reduced fertility and abortion in several species of animals and in human.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5bekg","text":"Camels are highly susceptible to Brucella abortu, and Brucella melitensis but they are not the primary hosts.  ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":33,"length":15},{"style":"italic","offset":54,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d8kmt","text":"Consumption of Brucellainfected milk and meat from camels is a serious public health issue though. Therefore rapid detection and diagnosis of ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":15,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dtn4m","text":"Brucellosis is crucial for any control program.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ir0a","text":"In this study, a sensitive and specific PCR assay was adapted for the detection and identification of Brucellaspp. in100 camels. Almost half of the collected samples were positive for Brucellaspp. (i. e. 41%).  44% of the samples were positive for Brucella abortus, 43% were positive for","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":102,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"erc7i","text":"B. melitensis, and 22% were positive for a co-infection of B. abortus andB.melitensis.The majority of infected camels were male adults.Most of ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":2,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":59,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":73,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fm24s","text":"the positive samples were collected at the slaughterhouse in Nyala. This ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dr63f","text":"finding is alarming as Sudanese consumers are known for eating raw ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3gqi4","text":"camels' liver. Therefore the main recommendation ofthis study is toadopt this sensitive and species-specific PCR protocol for routine ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2g3dp","text":"detection of  brucellosis in animals to be slaughtered in the future . ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8lq9i","text":"In the mean time this protocol can be used as a confirmatory test for the ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"193if","text":"detection ofbrucellosis in live camels as  well as for other animals to be slaughtered because it is the protocol with the lowest risk for laboratory ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"esccu","text":"personnel.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Wash 1ml of blood with Red Blood Cells lysis buffer (NaHCO3 0.0841g, NH4Cl6.15135g 1000ml distilled water) twice centrifuge at 60000 rpm for 5min

Same protocol is used to extract DNA from filter paper samples: add three 1cm pieces to 500µl lysis buffer (15.76gTris–HCl (pH 8), 10% SDS, and 1.8612gEDTA in 1000ml distilled water) leave overnight at 60 °C.
Add 500µl of Phenol/Chloroform/Isomyalcohol (25:24:1) to each sample then centrifuge for 5mn at 10000rpm ,
Transfer supernatant to another tube and add 500µl of Chloroform/Isomyalcohol (24:1)
Centrifuge at 100000rpm for 5min.
Add equal volume of 100 % ice cold ethanol and leave at -20°C overnight.
Centrifuge precipitated DNA at 14000 rpm for 10 min and wash with 70% ethanol two times.
Discard the supernatant and leave the tubes up- side-down to allow precipitated DNA to dry for 40min inside an incubator.
Dissolve the extracted DNA in 100µl double distilled water;
Quantify the concentration and store at -20°C.
Same protocol is used to extract DNA from milk samples except for the use of RBCS lysis buffer.
5 μl iNtRONMaxime™ PCR premix
2 µl each primer (step 15),
2 μl template DNA
95°C for 5 min;
35 cycle: 95°C for min, 65 °C for 1min, 72°C for ,1 min extension at 72°C for 7 min,
Final hold at 4°C.

Brucella spp. Detection primers (adopted from Khamesipouretal., 2014) B. abortus Forward primer: GACGAACGGAATTTTTCCAATCCC Reveres primer:TGCCGATCACTTAAGGGCCTTCAT Expected molecular size 494bp B. melitensis Forward primer: AAATCGCGTCCTTGCTGGTCTGA Reveres primer: TGCCGATCACTTAAGGGCCTTCAT Expected molecular size 743bp
Preheat lid to110°C,
Visualization : 2% (w/v) agarose gel containing 0.5× TBE buffer (743 mMTris–HCl (pH 8), 87 mM boric acid, and 5.3mM Na2EDTA), stained with ethidium bromide.
Add 1ml lysis buffer and leave at 60°C overnight.
creator: Rania Baleela
